entry.title: SAP ORD Made Simple: What It Does and Why It’s Crucial in your SAP Landscape + Purpose and Value
entry.content:
ORD (Open Resource Discovery) is a protocol and metadata specification developed  to enable standardized discovery and integration of business services, APIs, events, and other resources across distributed systems across SAP Products.


Find out more details on ORD : https://open-resource-discovery.org/introduction
Let’s break down what ORD is, why we created it, where it fits into your architecture, and how it adds value across your SAP landscape.

What Is SAP ORD (Open Resource Discovery)?

Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is a standardized protocol for metadata discovery that allows SAP applications and services to self‑describe their APIs, events, entity types, and other exposed resources. It provides a consistent, uniform structure for documentation, discovery, versioning, taxonomy, and lifecycle metadata.

Where ORD is Used in SAP

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Core component for service discovery and consumption
  • SAP S/4HANA Public & Private Cloud: Extensions and APIs are discoverable via ORD
  • SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) : Consumer & Publisher of ORD Metadata that requires in Data Products
  • SAP Integration Suite: Uses ORD for automatic API discovery
  • SAP Graph: Built on top of ORD for unified data access

ORD essentially acts as a "catalog of catalogs," making SAP’s complex ecosystem more navigable and integration-friendly.

How to Know If an SAP API Supports ORD (Open Resource Discovery)

One of the simplest and most reliable ways to verify whether an API supports Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is to check for the ORD ID directly on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com).

When an API is published with ORD metadata from each SAP Product, automatically displays an “ORD ID” in the API details pane. This confirms that the API is fully enabled for the standardized metadata structure provided by ORD, meaning it can be seamlessly discovered, crawled, and consumed by systems such as SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).

Where to Find the ORD ID (Screenshot Provided)

In the API detail screen — typically under Overview — you will see a section that lists:

  • Direction (Inbound / Outbound)
  • ORD ID (a unique standardized identifier representing the ORD resource)

In the example image provided, the lower-right information box contains:

  • Direction: Inbound
  • ORD ID: sap.s4:apiResource...


This clearly indicates that:

The API is published with ORD metadata and is ready for integration with SAP Business Data Cloud and its Data Products framework.

Why This Matters

Having an ORD-enabled API means:

  • Your integration can rely on standardized metadata such as entity types, relations, events, and API capabilities (as defined in SAP’s ORD specification).
  • SAP Business Data Cloud can easily discover and interpret the API’s metadata for creating or connecting to Data Products.
  • You avoid custom mapping or manual documentation because ORD ensures consistent structure and discoverability across SAP systems.

This is part of the broader SAP strategy — documented through the Open Resource Discovery protocol — to harmonize how APIs and services describe themselves across the SAP ecosystem.

Why ORD is Important in SAP

  1. Unified Integration: ORD provides a single, standardized way to describe and discover all integration resources (APIs, events, entity types) across SAP’s diverse product portfolio

  2. Automation: It enables automated discovery and consumption of services without manual documentation or configuration, significantly reducing integration effort

  3. Metadata Consistency: ORD ensures consistent metadata across systems, making it easier for developers and integration tools to understand and consume services

  4. Decentralization: Each application can expose its own ORD document describing its capabilities, eliminating the need for central catalogs that quickly become outdated

  5. Ecosystem Enablement: Partners, customers, and third-party tools can automatically discover what integration points are available in their SAP landscape

SAP Provides an Open‑Source ORD Repository for CAP Developers

To accelerate adoption of Open Resource Discovery (ORD) across custom applications, SAP also provides an open‑source GitHub repository that enables developers to implement ORD using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). This repository offers reference implementations, examples, and tooling support so developers, partners and customers can easily expose ORD‑compliant metadata from their own services.


https://github.com/cap-js/ord
 
Demo Application provides ORD

This makes it straightforward for CAP‑based applications to:

  • Self‑describe APIs, events, business objects, and data products
  • Register and publish metadata consistently with SAP’s ORD standard
  • Integrate cleanly into the broader SAP ecosystem, including SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Graph, and SAP BTP services

Conclusion

SAP ORD (Open Resource Discovery) is one of the most important but often overlooked components in SAP’s modern integration strategy. By standardizing metadata discovery, enabling automated publication, and providing a consistent structure across SAP products, ORD delivers massive value for architects, developers, and business teams.

If you’re working with SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Databricks or any other SAP landscapes, understanding and leveraging ORD is no longer optional—it’s essential.

entry.title: Re: Display of all business objects for maintenance order...
entry.content:
Hi,

You've pointed here one of the weaknesses of SAP standard regarding retrieval of documents. 

And to answer your requirement you have two options:

1/ Custom development, implement it yourself;

2/ Used a standard certified SAP solution: YAC Work Pack Manager is directly answering your requirement - Plug & play !

entry.title: Re: Mass Download Of Attachment In SAP Document / Invoice - SAP S4HANA / FICO
entry.content:
For S/4HANA Private Cloud / RISE and also on-premise, there exists an add-on able to do this "Plug & Play" for all types of attachments: ArchiveLink, GOS/KPro, DMS: YAC Work Pack Manager.

This add-on works "cross modules" so it can as well be used for mass download of attachments of any other object.

Furthermore for business domains such as plant maintenance, it's also able to retrieve all attachments of connected objects; e.g. you ask attachments of a maintenance order, it can also retrieve all the attachments of the operations, functional locations, equipments, notifications, etc. and download them all in one shot.

entry.title: Re: Automating the Import of ISO 20022 Customer Payment S...
entry.content:
Good day @EnricoZama 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, ISO 20022 Customer Payment Status Report (pain.002) are handled through the inbound payment message processing. A supported approach I recommend is to import these files through the Manage Incoming Payment Files. The App allows you to upload electronic payment related files including pain.002 in supported areas.

See the following references for more insights on how you can import payment files and process payment rejections:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/64c0d0a72b6e45679199805dc3167e5b.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2121275fa0824198b65bf1f4d71e5347/433583240e7242d4ac91b1619863e8f7.html?locale=en-US

With the Multi Bank Connectivity offered by SAP, you can navigate a full automation, where banks send status messages to SAP and the messages are then forwarded into the system for processing and monitoring in the payment lifecycle.

So, the recommended setup is either:

  1. You use Manage Incoming Payment Files for manual or semi automated imports, or
  2. You use SAP Multi Bank Connectivity to automate your inbound status message processing.

See the following references for more details.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3398869

advanced payment management: Processing Inbound Messages (PAIN.002) using Multi-Bank Connectivity

https://help.sap.com/doc/46118b42eda14211afd9d9d75c087c2e/Latest/en-US/f323d63678a747ccbcb3356faf958ffd.pdf

Here is a quick guide:

Open Manage Income Payment Files App,

Select the file type that matches your configuration and upload the pain.002 file.  After the import, review processing results in your payment monitoring apps.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Service Contract Cancellation Error
entry.content:
I am using ODATA V4 Service contract API for cancelling a service contract header. And sending this payload: 

{
    "ServiceContrCancellationReason": "V001",
    "ServiceContractCanclnParty": "CNVD",
    "SrvcContrCancellationDateTime": "2026-02-19T00:00:00Z"
}

 The first call was successful. Now, If I send another request it throws following error : 

{
    "error": {
        "code": "/IWBEP/CM_V4S_RUN/000",
        "message": "Unspecified provider error occurred. See Error Context and Call Stack.",
        "@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory": "Provider_Application_Error",
        "innererror": {
            "ErrorDetails": {
                "@SAP__common.Application": {
                    "ComponentId": "CRM-S4-SRV-CTR",
                    "ServiceRepository": "SRVD_A2X",
                    "ServiceId": "API_SERVICECONTRACT",
                    "ServiceVersion": "0001"
                },
                "@SAP__common.TransactionId": "6DFFBDC8ADC50270E0069A42188B54BC",
                "@SAP__common.Timestamp": "20260301112248.734489",
                "@SAP__common.ErrorResolution": {
                    "Analysis": "Use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details",
                    "Note": "See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)"
                }
            }
        }
    }
}


But why ? I have seen other service contract header cancellation API can be called multiple times. But here it is failing after first successful call. Any suggestion ? 

The service contract has following start and end date: 

    "ServiceContractStartDateTime": "2026-02-19T00:00:00Z",
    "ServiceContractEndDateTime": "2026-02-19T00:00:00Z",

entry.title: Service Contract Cancellation Error
entry.content:
I am using ODATA V4 API to cancel a service contract. The payload I sent looks like 

{
    "ServiceContrCancellationReason": "V001",
    "ServiceContractCanclnParty": "CNVD",
    "SrvcContrCancellationDateTime": "2026-02-19T00:00:00Z"
}

The request was successful at first attempt. But all consecutive request is failing with following error: 

{
    "error": {
        "code": "/IWBEP/CM_V4S_RUN/000",
        "message": "Unspecified provider error occurred. See Error Context and Call Stack.",
        "@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory": "Provider_Application_Error",
        "innererror": {
            "ErrorDetails": {
                "@SAP__common.Application": {
                    "ComponentId": "CRM-S4-SRV-CTR",
                    "ServiceRepository": "SRVD_A2X",
                    "ServiceId": "API_SERVICECONTRACT",
                    "ServiceVersion": "0001"
                },
                "@SAP__common.TransactionId": "6DFFBDC8ADC50270E0069A42188B54BC",
                "@SAP__common.Timestamp": "20260301112248.734489",
                "@SAP__common.ErrorResolution": {
                    "Analysis": "Use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details",
                    "Note": "See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)"
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

Not sure why it is not working. The current contract looks something like : 

    "ServiceContractStartDateTime": "2026-02-19T00:00:00Z",
    "ServiceContractEndDateTime": "2026-02-19T00:00:00Z",
    "ServiceContractCanclnParty": "CNVD",
    "ServiceContrCancellationReason": "V001",
    "SrvcContrCancellationDateTime": "2026-02-19T00:00:00Z",
    "ServiceContractCanclnSts": "C",

Any help is highly appreciated. Earlier I could send multiple requests even after cancellation without error.

entry.title: Extending existing widget with custom widget in wedget xml file
entry.content:
Hi there!I

I am currently working on extending an existing widget in the widgets.xml file by adding my custom widget.

I was able to successfully extend the hmc2 widget as shown below:

widgets.xml :

 

<widget-extension widgetId="hmc2">

<widget id="mySearch"

widgetDefinitionId="com.croma.web.backoffice.widgets.mySearch"

slotId="rightSlot"/>

<setting key="eastDisabled" type="Boolean">false</setting>

</widget-extension>


These changes are reflected correctly in Backoffice.

However, when I try to add my custom widget inside leftSlotContainer, which is part of hmc2, the changes are not being reflected.

I have tried both of the following approaches:

Approach 1:

<widget-extension widgetId="leftSlotContainer">
        <widget id="mySearch"
                widgetDefinitionId="com.croma.web.backoffice.widgets.mySearch"
                slotId="rightSlot"/>
        <setting key="northDisabled" type="Boolean">false</setting>
        <setting key="northHeight" type="String">200px</setting>
</widget-extension>

Approach 2:

<widget-extension widgetId="hmc2">
    <widget-extension widgetId="leftSlotContainer">
            <widget id="mySearch"
                    widgetDefinitionId="com.croma.web.backoffice.widgets.mySearch"
                    slotId="rightSlot"/>
            <setting key="northDisabled" type="Boolean">false</setting>
            <setting key="northHeight" type="String">200px</setting>
    </widget-extension>
</widget-extension>

Unfortunately, in both cases, the mySearch widget does not appear inside leftSlotContainer.

Could you please help me understand what might be missing or incorrect in my configuration?

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: Unable to access my certification attempt – no response from support and no case visibility
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am writing this post out of real frustration and disappointment.

I am a SAP Learning Hub – Student Edition subscriber (valid until May 30, with two certification attempts included). My goal is simple: I want to certify. I am fully prepared, motivated, and ready to take my system-based assessment.

However, I have been blocked by a technical issue that has not been resolved for days.

This is what happened:

• I attempted to launch my certification.
• I received the message: “Another certification is currently in progress.”
• I never started another session.
• I waited, logged out, cleared cache, tried different browsers, different devices. Same error.

I opened support cases via SAP Learning Support Team:

• 5000297480
• 5000300760

Support replied inside the portal, but now I cannot even see my own cases anymore. The system shows “No cases found” under my profile.

So at this point:

– I cannot launch the exam.
– I cannot see my support cases.
– I cannot reply to support.
– My subscription continues to expire.
– My certification attempts remain blocked.

I feel completely stuck and honestly ignored.

I am not asking for special treatment.
I just want access to the certification I paid for and prepared for.

Has anyone experienced something similar with the new system-based assessments?
Is there any SAP Education contact that can help escalate identity or portal visibility issues?

I would truly appreciate guidance.

Thank you

entry.title: GR Planning Version with Group Currency Different from Ledger Group Currency - FINCS_ACCTG_INTEG003
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting (On-Prem 2023) while working with a planning version that uses Plan Data Integration (ACDOCP).

Scenario:

  • Planning version with “Use Plan Data Integration” activated
  • Source Ledger: 0L
  • Company Code Local Currency = TRY
  • In ACDOCP, field HSL (Amount in Company Code Currency) is filled and the currency is TRY
  • Ledger Group Currency (Currency Type 30) = USD

Requirement:

The client would like to create a planning version with Group Currency = TRY.

Issue:

When running task RUJ, the system throws the error:

GC of version YBL and GC source currency for CoCd of company Q001 differ.

According to SAP documentation, for planning versions the system reads HSL or KSL from ACDOCP depending on the currency matching logic.

In my case:

  • HSL = TRY (matches local currency)
  • KSL = USD
  • Version Group Currency = TRY

However, the system still performs a validation against the ledger group currency (USD) and throws the error.

Is it technically possible to have a planning version with Group Currency different from the ledger group currency when using integrated plan data (ACDOCP)?

Or is the system design enforcing that:

Planning Version Group Currency must always equal the ledger group currency when “Use Plan Data Integration” is active?

Would the only supported approach be:

  • Set Version GC = Ledger GC (USD)
  • Use reporting/translation to display TRY?

Any clarification or confirmation of the expected system behavior would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Secure Data Flow for AI Inference on SAP BTP: RPT-1 vs LLM Orchestration
entry.content:
Really appreciate how clearly this blog explains the difference between RPT-1 and LLM Orchestration from a security and architectural standpoint.

 

What stood out to me is that this isn’t just about “using AI on BTP,” but about understanding the risk profile of each inference path and designing accordingly. The way identity, tenant isolation, and auditability are woven into the overall flow makes it practical for real enterprise scenarios.

 

For anyone working in regulated industries, this breakdown helps move the conversation from hype to responsible implementation. Very well articulated.

entry.title: Where ORD Is Used in SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud & SAP Databricks
entry.content:
SAP Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is becoming a foundational standard across the SAP ecosystem because it provides a consistent, self‑describing metadata layer for APIs, events, business objects, and data products. This metadata standardization is what enables smooth interoperability across SAP’s modern data and integration platforms.

Below are the key places where ORD is used today — and how it empowers seamless integration, especially with SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Databricks.

1. SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com)

SAP Business Accelerator Hub uses ORD as one of the primary mechanisms for publishing APIs and events. Any API showing an ORD ID on its overview page is officially ORD-enabled, meaning it exposes standardized metadata that can be consumed by SAP tools and data platforms.

This ensures:

  • Consistent API descriptions
  • Discoverability by SAP’s central aggregators
  • Compatibility with downstream systems like SAP Business Data Cloud
This is the first indicator that an API can participate in the broader data product ecosystem. 
 

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP uses ORD internally to improve SAP-to-SAP integration and provide a unified metadata layer that development tools (like SAP Business Application Studio) can consume for enhanced API discovery and automation.

 

3. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

SAP Business Data Cloud actively uses ORD to describe Data Products and make them discoverable to consumers across the SAP ecosystem, including SAP Datasphere. ORD helps BDC deliver a unified catalog with:

  • Descriptions of datasets
  • Relationships
  • Capabilities
  • Higher-level metadata structures like data products and business entities
In addition, third‑party systems such as SAP Databricks are required to describe data products using ORD before publishing them into SAP Business Data Cloud.
 
This ensures smooth catalog integration and consistent metadata across SAP and non-SAP data sources.
 

4. SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere consumes data products published to SAP Business Data Cloud — and because BDC requires ORD‑based metadata, Datasphere gains:

  • Automatically discoverable data products
  • Standardized metadata for modeling
  • Compatibility with external data such as Databricks Delta Lake

Datasphere uses ORD indirectly because it relies on BDC’s ORD-driven catalog for understanding and federating data assets.

Additionally, SAP’s hybrid integration patterns show that Datasphere can integrate with Databricks using JDBC or federated models — and ORD ensures data products between these systems are consistently described.

5. SAP Databricks (Enterprise Databricks Integration)

SAP Databricks integrates deeply with SAP Business Data Cloud using BDC Connect, enabling bi-directional sharing of data products. To publish a data product from SAP Databricks to SAP Business Data Cloud:

  • The metadata must be described using ORD and CSN (Core Schema Notation)
  • The data product becomes discoverable to SAP systems (e.g., Datasphere) via BDC

Furthermore, Databricks–SAP integration uses Delta Share, JDBC, and federated models, but ORD plays the critical role of standardizing data product metadata.

This creates a smooth, unified integration path:

SAP applications → ORD-based metadata → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere & Databricks

SAP Architecture Center also confirms this hybrid pattern where business data from SAP and non‑SAP (Databricks) is harmonized through Business Data Cloud and Datasphere, enabling interoperable AI/ML and analytics scenarios.

 Refer my SAP blog

6. Data Products Across the SAP Ecosystem

ORD also provides standardized metadata for:

  • Entity Types / Business Objects
  • APIs and Events
  • Data Products 
This consistency is the key enabler for cross‑platform interoperability — especially as Data Products become central to the SAP ecosystem via Business Data Cloud and Datasphere.

Summary: How ORD Enables Smooth Integration Across SAP & Databricks

With ORD in place:

  • APIs with an ORD ID on api.sap.com can be safely consumed to build data products in SAP Business Data Cloud.
  • Business Data Cloud uses ORD metadata to catalog and serve data products to SAP Datasphere and SAP Databricks.
  • SAP Databricks uses ORD when publishing its own data products back into Business Data Cloud.
  • SAP Datasphere leverages ORD-driven metadata to discover, model, and federate data products seamlessly.

ORD is the metadata “glue” that ensures smooth integration across:

SAP BTP → Business Accelerator Hub → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere → Databricks

And the more SAP and partner solutions adopt ORD, the smoother, more automated, and more scalable integrations become across the SAP landscape.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4Hana Public Cloud - Custom CDS View - How do I...
entry.content:
Hello @JJackson 

Apologies on the delayed response.

In Public Cloud, pricing condition records are generic, and their keys depend on the condition table, so a CDS like I_SlsPrcgConditionRecord/SlsPrcgConditionRecord will not always expose joinable key fields (for example Custoner Group 1 and 2) or direct associations to product text or sales cubes. See KBA 3682253 for more details.

Also, note that by design Materials is not available in CDS view I_SlsPrcgConditionRecord , and it recommended to use the released public API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV to retrieve sales pricing condition records including product related context. KBA 3332533 provides more insights.

So, if your goal is a current price list report that includes Condition Table, Customer Group 1 and 2, validity timeframe and condition amount, then he supported approach is as follows:

  1. You should extract the condition record master data with API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV and filter by validity (today between ValidFrom and ValidTo). See KBA 3521811 on how to get the condition data from the OData API.
  2. Enrich that data you extracted with product attributes like ID, text and weight with released product master CDS views, rather than trying to force a join to the generic pricing CDS which does not expose the required join fields. KBA 3332533 provides more insights.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud如何实现使用文档凭证功能，同时将制造图纸等文件保留在本地
entry.content:
Good day @JuliaLu 

Based on the details provided, below you will find some details.

The Public Cloud manages document handling through the Manage Documents app, which supports attachments, enables you version and document when the original files are stored in a connected content repository.

See the Blog: Document Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for more details.

If you do not want to upload the drawing files into S4 HANA Cloud, the only standard approach inside the app is to attach th drawing as a URL reference. This keeps the file outside SAP, but SAP will not manage the original file content like ECC DMS.

For a true enterprise solution where files stay in a customer owned repository (for example in Germany) while still being accessible from S4 HANA cloud, SAP supports third party repository integration for storing and reading business document from an external repository.

See the following details for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fe051b8031274f919dae94150971c621.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/03414f87d6e14dff9450e20e21632826.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: HOW TO FETCH DATA FOR THE LATEST RECORD FROM SAP TABLE USING CDS VIEW
entry.content:
Thanks. I understand the whole logic but not these few things:

  • The SQL function COALESCE is to be used if the column may contain NULL values, to replace null with any value you want, but the columns ERDAT and EZEIT of the table EKES should not be null as ABAP writes non-null values. Only native SQL insertions may write null values. In pure ABAP context, the most frequent reason is that the columns were added to the table while there were some records already in the table, if the "not null" DDIC attribute is false, they are then initialized with the null values automatically. Did you investigate why these table columns have null values and how many rows are concerned?
  • I get this syntax error for the view entity ZCDS_TEST: "No target type can be specified after addition AS in function CONCAT". The text "CAST( ... )" is missing around "coalesce(menge, 0.000) as abap.char(16)".
  • I get these syntax errors for the two key fields of the view entity ZCDS_TEST_JOIN:
    • "ebeln" is not unique since it is defined in "EKPO(WRF_PCON_EKKO_ADDON_STY)", "EKKO", and others
    • "ebelp" is not unique since it is defined in "EKPO(WRF_PCON_EKKO_ADDON_STY)" and "ZCDS_TEST"
    • Instead of "key ebeln, key ebelp," I must indicate "key a.ebeln, key b.ebelp,"
  • "=" may be used instead of "like '%...' " in "substring(c.max_date, 15, 8 ) like '%00000000' ", because it's equivalent to "substring(c.max_date, 15, 8 ) = '00000000' "
  • "c.max_date" may be used instead of "coalesce(c.max_date, '')" because "c.max_date" is never null
  • This is wrong: "cast(substring(c.max_date, 23, 16) as abap.dats)", and I get this warning: "CAST CHAR from to type : target type length too small, loss of data poss." It's non-sense to cast a substring of 16 characters which should contain EKES-MENGE casted as 16 characters, to a date.
    • To fix it, instead of abap.dats, I may refer to QUAN(13,3) or the data element BPMNG, but then I get this error because the quantity field doesn't refer to a unit of measure: "ZCDS_TEST_JOIN-MENGE reference information missing or data type wrong, see long text"
    • I can fix it by adding the element "b.meins" (EKPO)
    • Because of the cast to QUAN(13,3) or BPMNG, the part "THEN '0.000'", whose return type is of type character, is inconsistent with the ELSE CAST( ... ) which is of type QUAN(13,3), so '0.000' must be cast to QUAN(13,3) too. But then, it's easier to cast the whole CASE condition instead of its two branches THEN and ELSE.
  • Now, there's an issue concerning the mixed use of LEFT OUTER JOIN and INNER JOIN because without parentheses, it will behave like two INNER JOIN (issue well known in SQL, not limited to CDS). Parentheses must be added

The final code of ZCDS_TEST_JOIN is:

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'EKKO, EKPO and latest record of EKES'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}

define view entity zcds_test_join
  as select from    ekko       as a

    left outer join ( ekpo       as b
                      inner join    zcds_test   as c
                      on  b.ebeln = c.Ebeln
                      and b.ebelp = c.Ebelp )
      on a.ebeln = b.ebeln
{
  key a.ebeln,
  key b.ebelp,

      a.ernam,
      b.aedat,
      b.meins,

      cast(substring(c.max_date, 15, 8) as abap.dats)  as eindt,

      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'meins'
      cast(case when substring(c.max_date, 15, 8) = '00000000'
           then '0'
           else substring(c.max_date, 23, 16)
           end 
           as bpmng)     as menge
}

In my system, the table EKES is empty, so I can't test everything.

My system: ABAP 7.58 SP 1

entry.title: Blog Series: Agentic Reporting - The Next Evolution of SAP Reporting
entry.content:
Most enterprises still operate in a world of dashboards that simply report what happened. KPIs move, charts update, and alerts fire when thresholds are crossed — yet none of this tells us whether the deviation is expected, normal for this period, or a real operational risk.

With AI, reporting can evolve from passive visualization to active, context‑aware decisioning.
Instead of waiting for users to interpret numbers, the system itself can observe, understand context, reason over patterns, decide significance, and take action.

This is Agentic Reporting — where insights behave like a junior analyst embedded inside your SAP landscape (S/4HANA, SAC, Datasphere, BTP), continuously interpreting changes and surfacing only what truly needs attention.

What Makes Reporting Agentic? 

Traditional reporting is static: 

  • Predefined KPIs 
  • Rigid thresholds 
  • IF → THEN rules 
  • No learning  

Agentic reporting is dynamic: 

  •  Observes data continuously 
  • Considers context — seasonality, patterns, user role, business calendar 
  •  Determines if a deviation is meaningful 
  • Recommends or triggers action 
  • Learns from outcomes  

Why Agents > Classic Alerts/Rigid Triggers 

Reporting Triggers: 

  • Static 
  • One KPI - One Action
  • Same action every time 
  • Push Only
  • No Context, No learning  

AI Agents: 

  • Adaptive, context-aware thresholds 
  • Understands correlations across KPIs 
  • Chooses the optimal action 
  • Learns over time 
  • Proactive + conversational 

 

Agentic Reporting Use Case: KPI Breach Monitoring

Use Case: KPI Breach Monitoring Tool

Traditional Approach: 

  • “If DSO > 60 days → Send email.” 
  • No context → many false alarms. 

Agentic Approach: 

  • “Is this deviation abnormal given today’s reality?” 

 Scenario A – 8% DSO Increase (No Alert) 

Agent checks context

  • It’s the end of quarter (seasonal spike expected)
  • A major customer’s payment cycle always delays during this period
  • The same pattern occurred in the last 3 quarters

Conclusion: The deviation is expected and harmless → No alert raised.

 Scenario B – Same 8% Increase Next Month (Alert!) 

Agent checks context

  • No end of quarter ❌
  • No seasonal explanation ❌
  • pattern does not match past 3 quarters ❌
  • Impacts cashflow ❌
  • Unusual vs history ❌

Conclusion: The deviation is unexpected and risky → Escalate with analysis

 Agent provides: 

  • Root-cause patterns ✔️
  • Customer contribution ✔️
  • Impact on forecast ✔️
  • Recommended actions ✔️

 The Bottom Line 

Agentic Reporting takes organizations from: 

Dashboards you look at → Dashboards that look out for you. 

 As SAP deepens Joule, AI, and agentic capabilities, this shift becomes not only possible — but necessary.

entry.title: Understanding SAP HANA Disaster Recovery - Technical Scope, Distance, and Data Protection Boundaries
entry.content:
Introduction
SAP HANA provides documented mechanisms to support disaster recovery (DR) scenarios in which an entire site, data center, or availability zone becomes unavailable.

From a technical perspective, disaster recovery addresses failure domains that exceed the scope of local high-availability mechanisms. Its primary objective is to restore database operations after site-level outages, while preserving data consistency according to architecturally defined recovery objectives.

Disaster recovery is not a single feature. It is an architecture, built from documented mechanisms such as system replication, backup-based recovery, distance-aware configurations, and controlled takeover procedures.

This article consolidates SAP-documented SAP HANA disaster-recovery mechanisms into a single technical view.

Its purpose is to make scope, distance considerations, and data-protection boundaries explicit, supporting sound DR architecture design and precise technical discussions.

All statements are derived from official SAP Help Portal documentation.

1. Disaster Recovery in SAP HANA - Technical Definition
In SAP HANA documentation, disaster recovery refers to mechanisms that enable database availability after site-level failures, such as:

  • complete data-center outages
  • regional infrastructure failures
  • loss of connectivity between sites

Disaster recovery is explicitly distinct from:

  • High availability (HA)
    which minimizes downtime within a site or cluster
  • Backup and recovery
    which restore data to a previous state using backups and logs

This separation of objectives is consistently reflected in SAP HANA administration and architecture documentation.

Key boundary
Disaster recovery addresses failure domains, not individual components.

2. Failure Domains and Distance Considerations
A defining characteristic of disaster recovery is physical and logical separation between systems.

From a technical standpoint, DR architectures introduce:

  • network latency
  • increased failure isolation
  • asynchronous data-transfer requirements

Distance directly influences:

  • feasible replication modes
  • achievable Recovery Point Objective (RPO)
  • achievable Recovery Time Objective (RTO)

SAP HANA disaster-recovery mechanisms are therefore distance-aware by design.

Key boundary
Distance and latency are architectural constraints, not tuning parameters.

3. SAP HANA System Replication as a DR Mechanism
3.1 Technical Role in Disaster Recovery
SAP HANA System Replication (SR) is a primary building block for replication-based disaster recovery architectures.

In DR scenarios:

  • primary and secondary systems are deployed in separate sites
  • replication protects against site-level failures
  • takeover is initiated when the primary site is unavailable

System replication continuously transfers data changes from the primary system to the secondary system, maintaining a replica of the current database state.

SAP Help Portal reference
SAP HANA System Replication - Overview

3.2 Replication Modes and Distance
SAP HANA system replication supports synchronous and asynchronous replication modes.
From a disaster-recovery perspective:

  • Synchronous replication
    is typically constrained by distance and latency and is therefore limited to shorter distances
  • Asynchronous replication
    is commonly used for long-distance DR scenarios and accepts some replication lag

The selected replication mode directly influences:

  • potential data loss during failover
  • achievable RPO values

This behavior is an inherent consequence of documented SR semantics.

SAP Help Portal reference
SAP HANA System Replication - Replication Modes

Key boundary
Replication mode determines data-loss characteristics, not historical recovery capability.

4. Multi-Tier and Cascaded System Replication
SAP HANA supports multi-tier (cascaded) system replication, where data is replicated across more than one secondary system.

In disaster-recovery architectures, multi-tier replication can be used to:

  • combine high availability and disaster recovery objectives
  • separate near-site and far-site replicas
  • increase isolation between failure domains

Each replication tier operates within documented technical constraints, including supported replication paths and takeover rules.

SAP Help Portal reference
SAP HANA Multi-Tier System Replication
Supported Replication Modes Between Systems

Key boundary
Cascaded replication increases resilience, but does not replace backups.

5. Backup-Based Disaster Recovery
5.1 Technical Model
In addition to replication-based approaches, disaster recovery can be implemented using backup-based recovery.

In this model:

  • data and log backups are stored in a remote or isolated location
  • recovery is performed by restoring backups at the target site
  • point-in-time recovery is possible, depending on backup and log availability

Backup-based DR provides:

  • strong isolation between sites
  • access to historical restore points

It typically involves longer recovery times compared to replication-based DR.

SAP Help Portal reference
Planning Your Backup and Recovery Strategy

5.2 Replication-Based vs. Backup-Based DR
From a technical standpoint:

  • Replication-based DR
    prioritizes continuity with limited data loss
  • Backup-based DR
    prioritizes isolation and historical recovery

Most enterprise SAP HANA architectures combine both approaches to balance availability, data protection, and recovery flexibility.

6. Disaster Recovery vs. High Availability
Although both HA and DR may use system replication, their objectives and assumptions differ fundamentally.

  • High availability
    handles component-level failures within a site
  • Disaster recovery
    handles complete loss of a site or failure domain

Disaster recovery designs emphasize:

  • physical separation
  • controlled takeover procedures
  • explicitly defined data-loss boundaries

Key boundary
DR is validated against site-level failure scenarios, not host failures.

7. RPO and RTO in Disaster Recovery Context
In SAP HANA DR architectures:

  • RPO
    is influenced by replication mode or backup frequency
  • RTO
    is influenced by takeover procedures, recovery execution, and operational readiness

SAP HANA provides the technical mechanisms required to support different RPO/RTO targets.
Actual values are derived from architecture and configuration, not configured as product parameters.

8. Design Boundaries and Architectural Implications
From a disaster-recovery architecture perspective:

  • no single mechanism covers all site-level failure scenarios
  • replication-based DR trades isolation for continuity
  • backup-based DR trades speed for historical recovery
  • distance and latency impose hard technical constraints

Understanding these documented boundaries is essential for realistic DR expectations and technically sound architecture design.

Summary
SAP HANA provides documented technical mechanisms to support disaster-recovery scenarios involving site-level failures.

Disaster recovery is not defined by a single feature, but by the combination of system replication, backup mechanisms, distance-aware configuration, and controlled recovery procedures.

By understanding the documented scope, distance considerations, and data-protection boundaries of SAP HANA disaster-recovery mechanisms, architectures can be designed with clear, realistic, and technically defensible recovery expectations.

entry.title: Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users – make an impact by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) on https://influence.sap.com allow you, as an SAP customer or partner, to gain early insights into SAP's product developments and work directly with SAP teams to shape future product directions. This project presents a unique opportunity to work with SAPUI5 flexibility product managers and engineering teams, leveraging your expertise customizing SAPUI5 apps with key user adaptation.

Join the upcoming CEI project to make an impact on how key users interact with key user adaptation

If you’re already convinced – join here. If not, read on 🙂

What is key user adaptation?

Key user adaptation is a mode that allows extending SAPUI5 applications for other users right in the browser. It is highly intuitive and requires no technical or programming skills. Just use drag and drop to make UI changes – no modification or copy of SAP standard coding necessary! Read more here.

What is the targeted improvement?

Although the product is mature and widely used, we actively evolve it by delivering new functionality and capability enhancements alongside continuous user experience improvements, informed by UI testing and direct customer feedback. 

We are in the process of overhauling key user adaptation’s UI, simplifying the overall approach while making often-used scenarios more streamlined. We’ve taken a close look at the version history (of activated adaptations) and how our users interact with it, extended the “embed third-party content” feature, widened the scope of custom fields that can be added to the UI – and more.

To ensure that we are on the right way, we need your help!

Who should participate?

Users who want to adapt standard SAPUI5 apps for business users. Both key users and technical users are welcome.

Why should you participate?

  • Gain understanding of SAPUI5 flexibility
  • Chance to influence a central SAPUI5 feature
  • Early access to prototypes

How can you participate?

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to share your feedback and engage with SAPUI5 flexibility development teams. Register by March 15, 2026, and make a true impact on how you can customize SAP apps to your needs!

Click here to sign up for our project. (A short overview about how to get started on influence.sap.com can be found here).

After you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call where you will get more information. This call is followed by individual or grouped feedback sessions.

Thank you in advance for helping us shape the future of SAPUI5 flexibility!

entry.title: Re: Your 2026 Roadmap to Getting Started with ABAP AI and ABAP-1
entry.content:
@vsubbakrishna  Hey Subba, the ABAP AI SDK is our reuse library that enables you to integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) and generative AI features directly into your SAP business applications with ABAP. So it is the "AI toolbox," allowing you to build intelligent applications with business context in the ABAP stack. The ABAP AI SDK is available in:

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and OnPrem as of release 2025 via standard delivery.
  • S/4 HANA private cloud version and on-premise 2021 onwards are supported via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCI), see details in SAP Note 3513374.

Here you find more details: 

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-ai/generative-ai-in-abap-cloud/availability
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-blogs-posts/abap-meets-ai-my-hands-on-experience-with-sap-s-abap-sdk-for-llm/ba-p/14285453

entry.title: Re: Advanced Production Integration | Basic or Advanced E...
entry.content:
Hi @Martine 

This is a great question. The naming convention—'Advanced' Production Integration—is a frequent source of confusion because it sounds like it should automatically require the 'Advanced' EWM license.
​To clarify: Both Delivery-Based and PMR-based (Advanced) Production Integration are included in the Basic EWM license scope for SAP S/4HANA.
​The term 'Advanced' in this context refers to the technical depth of the integration (the use of the Production Material Request document to stay in sync with the manufacturing order) rather than a licensing tier. You only move into the Advanced EWM licensing bracket if you use specific 'extended' features such as Wave Management, Labor Management, Slotting, or the Material Flow System (MFS).
​You can find the official confirmation of this in the SAP S/4HANA Feature Scope Description. Under the 'Supply Chain' section for Warehouse Management, 'Production Integration' is listed as a core capability.
​Direct Reference Link: SAP S/4HANA Feature Scope Description (PDF)
(See Section 3.2.3.1 for Basic Warehouse Management capabilities).

entry.title: An AI Tool That Turns SuccessFactors $metadata Into a Clean Excel Data Dictionary in 30 Secs
entry.content:
If you've ever opened a SuccessFactors $metadata XML file in a browser and felt your soul leave your body, this post is for you.

Whether it's an Employee Central rollout or a complex payroll integration there's one task that shows up on every single project and nobody enjoys doing it: documenting the data model.

Every implementation needs a data dictionary. Every integration needs a field mapping sheet. Every security audit asks "what fields exist, what's required, who can edit what?" And the answer to all of these lives in one place: the OData $metadata endpoint. A single XML file that is, on average, 50,000 lines of raw schema definition that no human should ever have to read manually.

So I built a tool to fix that. It uses Claude AI to parse any SuccessFactors $metadata XML file and transform it into professional, color-coded Excel workbooks, Word documents, CSV exports, and even instance comparison reports — in about 30 seconds.

Let me show you how it works.

The Problem: 50,000 Lines of This

Here's what you see when you open https://your-instance.successfactors.com/odata/v2/$metadata in a browser:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<edmx:Edmx Version="1.0" xmlns:edmx="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/06/edmx">
  <edmx:DataServices m:DataServiceVersion="2.0">
    <Schema Namespace="SFOData" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2008/09/edm">
      <EntityType Name="EmpJob" sap:label="Job Information">
        <Key>
          <PropertyRef Name="seqNumber"/>
          <PropertyRef Name="startDate"/>
          <PropertyRef Name="userId"/>
        </Key>
        <Property Name="seqNumber" Type="Edm.Int64" Nullable="false"
          sap:label="Sequence Number" sap:creatable="false" sap:updatable="false"
          sap:filterable="true" sap:sortable="true"/>
        <Property Name="startDate" Type="Edm.DateTime" Nullable="false"
          sap:label="Start Date" sap:creatable="true" sap:updatable="true"
          sap:filterable="true" sap:sortable="true"/>
        <Property Name="userId" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="100" Nullable="false"
          sap:label="User ID" sap:creatable="true" sap:updatable="false"
          sap:filterable="true" sap:sortable="true"/>
        <Property Name="company" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="128"
          sap:label="Company" sap:creatable="true" sap:updatable="true"
          sap:filterable="true"/>
        <Property Name="costCenter" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="128"
          sap:label="Cost Center" sap:creatable="true" sap:updatable="true"/>
        <Property Name="department" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="128"
          sap:label="Department" sap:creatable="true" sap:updatable="true"/>
        <Property Name="cust_workShiftCode" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="255"
          sap:label="Work Shift" sap:creatable="true" sap:updatable="true"
          sap:filterable="true"/>
        <!-- ... 80+ more fields ... -->
        <NavigationProperty Name="companyNav" Relationship="SFOData.EmpJob_company"
          FromRole="EmpJob" ToRole="FOCompany"/>
        <NavigationProperty Name="departmentNav" Relationship="SFOData.EmpJob_department"
          FromRole="EmpJob" ToRole="FODepartment"/>
        <!-- ... 30+ more navigation properties ... -->
      </EntityType>
      <!-- ... 500+ more entity types ... -->
    </Schema>
  </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

Now multiply this by 500+ entities. That's your $metadata file. Finding "which fields on EmpJob are required and creatable via API" means scrolling through thousands of lines, cross-referencing Nullable="false" with sap:creatable="true", and manually building a spreadsheet.

I've done this. You've done this. We've all done this. And it takes hours.

The Solution: Upload, Ask, Done

Here's the new workflow. It takes three steps.

Step 1: Download Your $metadata

Navigate to your instance's metadata endpoint and save the XML:

 
 
https://your-instance.successfactors.com/odata/v2/$metadata

Save as metadata.xml. This file is typically 5–15 MB depending on your instance configuration.

Step 2: Upload to Claude and Ask

Open Claude (claude.ai or Claude Code) and upload the file. Then just ask for what you need in plain English:

"Parse this SuccessFactors metadata and create an Excel data dictionary with a summary sheet, individual sheets for each Employee Central entity, and a master list of all fields."

Step 3: Get Your Data Dictionary

In about 30 seconds, Claude generates a professional multi-sheet Excel workbook. Here's what each sheet looks like:

What You Get: The Excel Output

Summary Sheet

The first sheet gives you a bird's-eye view of your entire instance:

📊Your SF Data Dictionary is Complete!

Here's what I created for you:

Workbook Contents:

  1. Legend (Quick Reference)
    • Color coding guide
    • Column definitions and what they mean
    • Helps you understand the data at a glance
  2. Summary (Executive Overview)
    • 726 total entities
    • 13,469 total fields across all entities
    • 3,965 associations (relationships between entities)
    • 82 complex types
    • Breakdown by category (Employee Central, Position Management, Compensation, etc.)
  3. All Fields (Master Data Dictionary)
    • Every single field from every entity
    • Sortable and filterable
    • Includes: field name, type, required status, max length, labels, creatable/updatable flags
    • Color-coded for quick identification:
      • Yellow = Key fields (primary identifiers)
      • Light Red = Required fields (must have a value)
      • Light Blue = Custom fields (created via MDF)
  4. Entity-Specific Sheets (Top 30 Entities)
    • Detailed breakdown for the most important entities
    • Includes navigation properties
    • Ready for deep-dive analysis
  5. Navigation Properties (3,529 Relationships)
    • Shows how entities connect to each other
    • From/To entity mappings
    • Essential for understanding data flow and integration points

Use This For:

  • 📋Data Mapping — Connect SF fields to your payroll, ATS, or other systems
  • 🔍Field Discovery — Quickly find what fields exist and what they do
  • 📖Compliance & Documentation — Audit-ready reference for your instance
  • 🔗Integration Planning — Understand relationships between entities
  • 👥Team Communication — Non-technical stakeholders can understand the schema

You can now download, share, and use this in Excel with full filtering, sorting, and pivot table capabilities!

Real-World Use Cases

Let me walk through five scenarios I've personally used this tool on.

Use Case 1: New Implementation Data Dictionary

The ask: "We need a complete data dictionary for our Employee Central go-live documentation."

The prompt:

"Parse this metadata and create an Excel workbook. I need a summary sheet with all entities grouped by SF module, and detailed field sheets for: User, EmpEmployment, EmpJob, EmpCompensation, PerPersonal, PerEmail, PerPhone, PerNationalId, and PerAddressDEFLT. Also include a navigation properties sheet."

Time to deliver: 30 seconds instead of 2–3 days of manual work.

What I used to do: Open the metadata XML in Notepad++, Ctrl+F for each entity, manually copy field names into Excel, cross-reference attributes one by one. For a typical EC implementation with 10 core entities, this was a solid 2-day task.

Use Case 2: Integration Field Mapping

The ask: "Map all creatable fields on EmpJob to our payroll system — we need field name, label, type, and max length."

The prompt:

"From this metadata, create a CSV with all fields on EmpJob where sap:creatable is true. Include columns: Field Name, Label, Data Type, Max Length, Required. Sort by required fields first."

The result: A clean CSV file that the integration team can immediately import into their mapping tool. No manual parsing needed.

Use Case 3: Dev vs. Prod Comparison

This one is my favorite. After a series of transports, the client wanted to verify that their Production instance had all the same custom fields as Development.

The prompt:

"I've uploaded two metadata files — metadata_dev.xml and metadata_prod.xml. Compare them and show me: entities that exist in one but not the other, and custom fields (cust_) that differ between instances."*

The output: A comparison report showing:

  • 3 custom MDF objects existed in DEV but hadn't been transported to PROD
  • 7 custom fields on EmpJob were in PROD but missing from DEV (someone configured directly in Prod — a compliance issue!)
  • 2 fields had different Nullable settings between environments

This kind of configuration drift is a real problem in SF implementations, and this tool catches it instantly.

Use Case 4: Security Audit — "What Can This API User Access?"

The ask: "For our security review, we need to document every field that is filterable and sortable across all entities — these represent potential data extraction vectors."

The prompt:

"Parse this metadata and create an Excel sheet showing all fields across all entities where sap:filterable = true. Group by entity, and add a column showing whether the field contains PII (look for fields with labels containing 'name', 'email', 'phone', 'address', 'national', 'birth', 'gender', 'salary')."

This delivers a ready-made appendix for your security audit documentation.

Use Case 5: Quick Lookup During a Call

Not every use case needs a full export. Sometimes you're on a call with a client and they ask: "Is the businessUnit field on EmpJob required? Can we update it via API?"

The prompt:

"From this metadata, tell me about the businessUnit field on EmpJob — is it required, creatable, updatable, and what's the max length?"

Claude's response:

The businessUnit field on EmpJob is:

  • Type: Text (Edm.String), Max Length 128
  • Required: No (Nullable = true)
  • Creatable: Yes
  • Updatable: Yes
  • Filterable: Yes
  • Label: "Business Unit"

Answer delivered in 5 seconds while you're still on the call.

How to Get Started

Option 1: Use It Right Now in Claude.ai

  1. Go to claude.ai,
  2. Follow instructions provided on GitHub.
  3. Upload your $metadata XML file (download from your instance)
  4. Type your request: "Parse this SuccessFactors metadata into an Excel data dictionary"
  5. Download the generated Excel file

That's it. No installation, no configuration, no code.

Option 2: Install as a Claude Code Plugin

If you use Claude Code (Anthropic's terminal-based coding assistant), you can install this as a permanent plugin:

 

bash
/plugin install shaktibarath/sf-metadata-parser

Once installed, Claude automatically recognizes when you're working with SF metadata and applies the skill.

Option 3: Use via the Claude API

For automated pipelines (e.g., nightly metadata snapshots), you can call the skill programmatically via the Claude API with the Skills endpoint.

How to Download Your $metadata

If you haven't done this before, here's where to find it:

  1. URL format: https://<api-server>/odata/v2/$metadata
  2. Authentication: Use your SF admin credentials (Basic Auth) or an API user
  3. Example API servers by data center:
    • DC2: api2.successfactors.eu
    • DC4: api4.successfactors.com
    • DC8: api8.successfactors.com
    • DC10: api10.successfactors.com
    • DC12: api12.successfactors.com
    • DC55: api55.successfactors.eu
  4. Save as: Right-click → Save As → metadata.xml

Or use the browser developer tools (Network tab) if your instance requires SSO.

For a filtered metadata (specific entities only), append ?$filter= but note that the full metadata is more useful for comprehensive documentation.

What's Next

This tool is open-source on GitHub and I'm actively developing it. If you're an SF consultant, admin, or integration developer — I'd love your feedback. What outputs would save you the most time? What's missing?

Try It Now

The tool is available today:

  • Claude.ai — Upload your metadata XML and start asking questions
  • GitHub — https://github.com/shaktibarath/sf-metadata-parser
  • Claude Code — /plugin install shaktibarath/sf-metadata-parser

If it saves you even one afternoon of manual XML parsing, it was worth building.

Have questions or feedback? Find me on LinkedIn or open an issue on GitHub.

entry.title: How to exclude agents from approval steps in ABAP Platform workflows
entry.content:
There are several use cases where you want to exclude people from the processing of a particular work item.

“Excluded agents”

Therefore a feature called “Excluded Agents” is available. Excluded agents are the people you don’t want to act on a particular work item, even though they are possible or responsible agents.

One use case are approval workflows, which are often built to guarantee compliance and enforce processes to include more than one person to accomplish the outcome. Examples are requests for payments or orders, that should be reviewed by a manager or expert, who approves or rejects the request. However, if an employee has created the request, he should not be able to approve its own request. You therefore want to exclude that employee.

Another use case are processes where you want to ensure, that at least two (or even more) agents of a team review a request. You send the first work item to review a request to all agents in this team. Once the first work item is completed, you set the agent who processed it as the excluded agent for the next work item. Doing so ensures that the second work item is sent to all team members except the first approver. As a result, the same agent cannot process the request more than once.

Both use cases can be implemented using Excluded Agents. They are defined through an expression in the workflow step. In other words, you create a list (a multiline container element) containing all agents to be excluded, and then assign this list to the workflow step.

Design Time

You need to define a multiline container element for excluded agents.

As data type take "SWP_AGENT".

 To directly include the initiator mark the import and the multiline checkbox.

 Use the container element to exclude agents in a activity agent determination.

The “normal” case

Normally, the workflow initiator is added to the list of excluded agents as requester to ensure that the 4 eyes principle is respected.

Implementation via container operation

Bind the workflow initiator to the excluded agent list during start of the workflow

Create a binding from the event to store the initiator (can also be added to a multiline container element directly)

Substitutes

In case the requester is a substitute for the approver the need is the same. To avoid excluded agents are hindered to approve requests as substitutes as well.

Forwarding

In case an approver forwards the workitem to the requester the need is the same. To avoid excluded agents are hindered to approve requests over forwarding, this is also checked via execution of an item.

Multi-Step Approvals

In a three step (6 eyes principle) approval, it might be relevant to add previous approvers to the list of excluded agents, so a first level approver is not allowed to approve again on the second level.

              Implementation via Binding / Container Operation

To add an approval in a workitem to the list, so that a second approval is not possible, bind the actual agent to the list of excluded agents. Pay attention to choose the binding statement "Append to table" depicted by the << symbol.  

 

 Another special case – rework after rejection

Let's look at another typical use case. Once a workitem has been rejected, or if the rejected workitem respectively the document has been reworked, then in the subsequent approval process, all responsible agents except the workflow initiator (or the actual agent changing the document or both) should again be considered as potential agents. This requires rebuilding the list of excluded agents. 

One way to achieve this is to make use of a container operation in the rework step.

Add the workflow initiator to the rework step task container.

Clear the list of excluded agents after rework (using a modelled container operation or the binding of the rework task) and bind only the workflow initiator again to the excluded agent list. Pay attention to choose the binding statement "Assign Value" depicted by the < symbol. With this you ensure, that only the desired agent is in the list afterwards.

Solution for excluded agents via coding in callbacks and step properties for flexible workflows

There is also an implementation to handle excluded agents via coding by procurement colleagues. This is controlled by a property which is set in Manage Workflows: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/bacc21e14d6c4b0fbf53dca3218abdd5.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Additional Remarks / Disclaimer

Please note that the list of excluded agents is only evaluated at creation time of the work item. This means, that changes to the container element used for excluded agents do not take effect on already created work items. This in turn means, that for parallel/multi-instance work items, it is not possible to add agents/users to the list of excluded agents when the work items were already created. So other measures need to be taken to avoid processing of multiple work items by the same user. 

Further please note, that the feature of excluded agents does not hinder users with special privileges to use administrative tools of the workflow environment to process work items. Please do also see note 2478354 - Authorization to execute a work item .

entry.title: Re: JMS Mastery Series Part 3 - JMS in Production
entry.content:
@MateuszPiotrowski Thank you very much for a great blog series!  I wonder how other SAP customers deal with the switch from PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite as for example quality of service (BE/EO/EOIO) seemed in PI/PO more of an architectural challenge as described in your blog, but not an implementation challenge as it feels to me in Cloud Integration.

Would be great if others could comment/share their experience on:

  1. We try to avoid using the Cloud Integration XI adapter for connections from/to SAP even though it has some nice features, but it seems like SAP is focusing more on the SOAP adapter.. This means we no longer see our synchronous messages (with their payloads) in SAP ERP as it seems impossible to activate such logging for SRT_MONI but can be done for SXI_MONITOR.
    • Do others use Cloud Integration XI adapter? Is this a more permanent solution intended for Cloud Integration customers and not just those migrating off PI/PO?
    • How do others handle the payload logging, especially of synchronous messages in ERP or S/4? It is nice from a support perspective to see also payloads for successful messages, but doing that in Cloud Integration seems not advisable given storage limits and flat (non-interface-specific) retention periods.
  2. EOIO with message-content-specific queue names was easy in PI/PO, but seems impossible via JMS queues in Cloud Integration. What do others use for asynchronous interfaces that require EOIO with transaction/object id level queue names? (Especially for those connecting from/to an SAP system like SAP ERP or S/4, where this seems easy and handled by the frameworks.) Seems to me like a lack of a good EOIO option could in some scenarios be an argument for synchronous processing.

Thank you again for a very detailed and educational post!

entry.title: Negative ATP After Storage Location Transfer (323) Involving Quality Inspection Stock
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

This issue has surely been raised before, but I’ll give it a try anyway.

I have a material XXXX with stock available in inventory:

  • Batch X1 with 306 units (Unrestricted Use)
  • Batch Y1 with 3,963 units (Quality Inspection)

ATP parameter: ZP (Plant Stock)

In CO09 for material XXXX, only the available unrestricted stock is shown.

We need to perform transfers between storage locations when the stock is in Quality Inspection status. After executing the transfer posting (MIGO, movement type 323), an outbound delivery is automatically created. However, this delivery ends up blocking all the stock (both Unrestricted Use and Quality Inspection), resulting in a negative Cumulative ATP Quantity.

This situation prevents the system from confirming available stock in sales orders.

Question:
How can we prevent the ATP check applied on the outbound delivery from considering Quality Inspection stock in a way that blocks the Unrestricted Use stock?

entry.title: Re: S-user ID is about to expire need Validity Extension
entry.content:
To extend the expiry date for your S-user ID, you will need to contact one of the existing administrators within your company. Administrators are responsible for managing and updating expiration dates for S-user IDs. Unfortunately, SAP Support is unable to make changes to the expiration date of S-user IDs directly.

To identify the administrators associated with your customer number, please refer to the My Important Contacts(https://me.sap.com/userscontacts/impcont) section available on SAP for Me. For your convenience, here are two helpful resources:
2971742 - How to extend the Expiration Date of your own S-user ID - SAP for Me
2930885 - How administrators manage the Expiration Date for other S-user IDs - SAP for Me
If you need further assistance or have any additional questions, please feel free to reach out. 
Best Regards,  
Judy  
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Tickets of System:CLOUD
entry.content:
As per your description, I understand you would like to change the case reporter .

Regarding to your request, please refer to KBA 2403394 - How to change support case details - SAP for Me.

Abstract below information for your reference:

Once a support case has been submitted to SAP it is not possible to change the following details:

S-user ID (reporter)

In other words, the case reporter is selected by the customer when they submit the case, and once the case is created, the reporter cannot be changed.

If the S-user wants to follow up on the case, one workaround I advise is to add an additional contact according to the KBA 2213199 - How to add/remove additional contacts in an existing case - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: new s-user to raise tickets
entry.content:
S-users are managed by the administrators within your company. To help you resolve this, could you please follow the steps below:

1.Contact one of your company’s existing administrators via https://me.sap.com/userscontacts/impcont and request the creation of a new S-user ID for the new user.  

2.Ensure that the necessary authorizations are assigned to the newly created S-user ID by your administrator. These should include permissions for support case handling and the 'Display Cloud Data' authorization for the Successfactors installation.

1271482 - How does an administrator create or delete S-user IDs? - SAP for Me
3383142 - Which S-user ID authorizations are required for support case handling? - SAP for Me

If you have any additional questions or need further assistance, please do not hesitate to reach out. I am here to help.  

Best Regards,  
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SCORM packages generated from SEN producer are not wo...
entry.content:
Hi Nagaraj,

A 404 error typically indicates that some content is not available with SF.  This could be because the actual content to be displayed has not been included in the SCORM package when generated, however, if you indicate that the same SCORM package is working in SCORM Cloud, perhaps we have another issue.

It looks to me like you are trying to open a Simulation in SF.  In Producer, click the > in front of the Simulation name so that you can see the objects that represent the different Simulation modes.  Please make sure that at least one of those modes is visible, if you have hidden them all, you could well get a 404 error.

If at least one Simulation mode is visible, please raise a support ticket using Component ID KM-SEN-CMP and get the support team to take a look in detail.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Set up the Basic Trial account and add the ABAP environment in ADT that is enabled with Joule for De
entry.content:
The Basic Trial offering for SAP Build has been extended with a new workbook called 'Fusion Development with ABAP Cloud in SAP Build'. You can explore this workbook for the Basic Trial offering around SAP Build, that shows how developers can now create new ABAP Cloud projects, or work on existing ones, from the SAP Build lobby and then navigate to their ABAP development environment – the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT).

Task 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account and add the ABAP instance in ADT.

Click the following URL:   

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/build/trial.html

In the following screen click on "Try Now". 

You will receive a user with a password that will allow you to authenticate at the landing page of the basic trial in the respective region for your user, e.g. https://trials.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/ .  

Basic Trial Registration

By using the credentials presented in the web page you will get access to some workbooks that cover different scenarios. 

The ABAP related workbook is named Fusion Development with ABAP Cloud in SAP Build.

Select the highlighted workbook.

Choose the Highlighted Workbook

Click ‘Start Workbook’

Start WorkBook

Note : It may take up to 30 minutes to fully activate your user account in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. 

Read the details mentioned in Overview of the Section ‘Set up your development environment’. You can skip Step 1 and 2 if you already have ADT installed but make sure you are on the latest version of ADT for all the features to work fine. Once the installation is done directly move to Step 3 - 'Create ABAP Cloud Project in SAP Build' only until adding the basic trial instance to ADT. We will be it using the URL marked in the below screenshot.

Note:- Once you have the system added in ADT you can close the workbook.

System URL

Task 2 :  Create a package in the new ABAP Cloud Project 
Once you see the new ABAP Cloud Project (TDI ) added in ADT, create a package with the naming convention ZAC_<your community ID>.

To get the community ID launch the URL : https://community.sap.com/ and click on the person icon as shown in below screenshot and go to ‘My Content’.

Community Page

Once the below page opens up please copy the number after user-id in the URL to get your community ID.

Community ID

Note:- We will be validating your activities by checking the folder against your community ID. So make sure all your development objects are saved inside this package.

Task 3 :  Learn about the SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities

To prepare for the challenge familiarise yourself with the scope first by going through the following help document.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/joule-for-developers-abap-ai-capabilities-f14ebffef77b41bfb0746c33dcb70e84?locale=en-US

Now you are ready to take up the challenge. See you next week !!

entry.title: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
entry.content:
Let’s kick off this year’s Developer Challenge with a topic close to home: ABAP.

With AI dominating conversations across the tech world, we thought it would be exciting for our community to explore Joule for Developers this month.

SAP Joule for Developers brings embedded AI capabilities to SAP Build and ABAP, delivered through Joule. It helps accelerate the development of apps and extensions, reduce development costs, and enhance overall developer productivity and proficiency.

SAP Joule for Developers is engineered for ABAP excellence by

  • Leveraging SAP’s ABAP large language model trained on enterprise-grade code.
  • Generating data models, app logic, and unit tests with precision.
  • Migrating custom ABAP code faster with documentation chat, code explanation, and issue diagnostics.

The Challenge:

  For this challenge we will be using Basic Trial offering for SAP Build that includes a SAP BTP ABAP Environment with Joule for Developers. During the first week we will focus on setting up the ABAP instance from the Basic Trial in ADT and then will explore some of the key functionalities of Joule for Developers.

How to Participate:

To participate in and complete this challenge, simply follow these steps:

  • Complete the challenge assigned for each week.
  • Create a package with your community ID in the format mentioned in the Week1 challenge.
  • Post your package name in the thread for each week.
  • We will review your work inside the package with your community ID.

Challenge Timeline

The challenge will span for 4 weeks starting on Monday March 2nd 2026 and ending on March 23rd 2026. We will keep the challenge open till the end of the month to accomodate anyone who is running late 🙂. To count towards the overall monthly challenge, you must complete and submit each week's task. Make sure to revisit this blog post to discover the upcoming week's challenge!

March 2nd, 2026   – Start of Challenge: Week 1

March 9th, 2026    –  Week 2 :

March 16th, 2026  – Week 3 :

March 23rd, 2026  – Week 4 :

So let’s get started.

Rewards

Upon submitting all required entries by the end of the challenge, you'll earn an SAP Community badge for participating in the SAP Developer Challenge for March. Please ensure to follow all the instructions mentioned in this blogpost or the respective challenge discussions.

We are excited to hear your experience about the challenge and the learning adventure.

Best of Luck !!!
